steps.title: Find the needed generalization
steps.content: The student wants a generalization about how word order is used across languages. The useful note is that 87% of languages are classified as either SVO or SOV.

steps.title: Connect the shared pattern
steps.content: Both SVO and SOV orders place the subject before the verb and object. Since those two categories include 87% of the world's languages, the notes support a broad claim about the majority of languages.

steps.title: Avoid definitions and methods
steps.content: Choices A and B focus on linguistic typologists' classification work, and Choice C only defines word order. The remaining choice states the supported usage pattern. The answer is D.
